race.name: White Pine Stampede
race.tagline: 42 kilometers point-to-point through northern Michigan's frozen forests — the state's oldest and most iconic Nordic marathon since 1977.

vitals.aid_stations: Aid stations along the course with warm drinks and food

climate.description: Early February in northern Michigan delivers cold, snowy conditions enhanced by Lake Michigan lake-effect snow. Temperatures typically range from -15°C to -3°C. The region receives abundant snowfall, though recent warm winters have occasionally threatened conditions.
challenges: Lake-effect snow can dump heavy snow before or during the race
Sub-zero wind chills on exposed sections
Variable snow conditions from lake-effect moisture
Recent warm winters have challenged snow coverage

course.primary: Point-to-point from Mancelona to Bellaire through northern Michigan's forests and rolling terrain. The course follows a mix of old railroad grades and forest trails with moderate elevation changes. Classic northern hardwood forest scenery throughout.
course.surface: Machine-groomed trails through northern Michigan forest
course.grooming: Good machine grooming with classic tracks and skating lane
features: Northern Michigan's oldest point-to-point Nordic marathon
Route through classic northern hardwood and pine forests
Multiple distance options for different ability levels
Strong community volunteer support along the course

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: A beloved Michigan institution with nearly 50 years of history. Strong community character and the point-to-point format give it appeal, but moderate terrain, low altitude, and limited international draw keep it mid-Tier 3.

history.summary: The White Pine Stampede has been northern Michigan's premier Nordic ski marathon since 1977. As the state's oldest point-to-point XC race, it holds a special place in the Midwest skiing community. The race takes its name from the white pine forests that once dominated northern Michigan before the logging era. For nearly five decades, it has drawn skiers to the quiet beauty of Michigan's northern Lower Peninsula.
notable_facts: Northern Michigan's oldest point-to-point Nordic marathon since 1977
Named for the white pine forests of northern Michigan's logging heritage
Point-to-point format from Mancelona to Bellaire
Strong volunteer tradition — local community organizes the entire event
Offers both classic and freestyle in multiple distances
Part of the Michigan Cup racing series
